mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 8.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, prompting risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts (HOUST) and real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Joint growth in HOUST and RSAFS coincides with SPX negative 60d returns in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing activity, which historically correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict SPX returns with Rank IC > 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and elevated policy rates (FEDFUNDS) signals a tightening cycle, which historically correlates with negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through higher discount rates.
failure_condition: Joint increases in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS coincide with SPX positive 20d returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 130k indicates weak labor market performance, which historically correlates with negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through reduced consumer spending expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS falls below 130k.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) above 2% signals strong macroeconomic momentum, which historically correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings outlook.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict SPX returns with Rank IC > 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low inflation (CPIAUCSL < 2%) and low policy rates (FEDFUNDS < 1%) signals accommodative monetary conditions, which historically correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through lower risk-free rates.
failure_condition: Joint low CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS coincide with SPX negative 20d returns in > 40% of cases.
